lenses.guidance: Moses pleads 'I am slow of speech and tongue' (v 10) — Hebrew kebed peh ukebed lashon, literally 'heavy of mouth and heavy of tongue'. The original audience hears physical impediment, not stage fright; grace works through real disability.

Moses' near-fatal encounter at the inn (vv 24-26) and Zipporah's circumcision act preserve the deliverer for the deliverance. The covenant sign cannot be neglected by the very man God will use to lead a circumcised people.

The unsettling 'meeting' at the inn (vv 24-26) echoes earlier Jacob-Peniel wrestling (Gen 32:24-32) — encounters of judgement that reshape the covenant-bearer rather than destroy him. A pattern across Scripture.
Moses gives five objections (vv 1, 10, 13) and God answers each with provision — signs, Aaron, the rod. Faith is not the absence of objection but obedience past objection. Pray your fears, then go.
